name: Baladeva Vidyabhushana
dates_notes: Late Mughal / early colonial; dates well-established by manuscript and inscriptional record.
core_thesis:
Baladeva Vidyābhūṣaṇa is the formal Vedāntist of the Gauḍīya school. The Six Gosvāmīs' *acintya-bhedābheda* is preserved without doctrinal alteration — Kṛṣṇa as *svayaṃ-bhagavān*, the threefold *śakti* with *acintya-śakti* as load-bearing concept, the *jīva* as eternal *taṭastha-aṃśa*, *bhakti* and *prema* as *abhidheya* and *prayojana*. The contribution is the institutional achievement the school had previously lacked: a complete Brahma-Sūtra commentary in classical Vedāntic form.

The doctrinal framing draws openly on the Madhva *paramparā*. Baladeva was trained in Mādhva Vedānta before his Gauḍīya commitment, and the *Govinda-Bhāṣya* accordingly reads with a Dvaita-inflected emphasis on the irreducibility of *bheda* — clearer in places than the Gosvāmīs themselves had pressed it — while preserving the *acintya-śakti* qualification that distinguishes the Gauḍīya position from straight Madhva-Dvaita. The hybridity is structural rather than incidental, and Baladeva makes it explicit by formally embedding the Gauḍīya line in the *brahma-mādhva-gauḍīya paramparā*.

With the *Prameya-Ratnāvalī* and the *Siddhānta-Ratnam*, the school's doctrinal profile is set out in the standard scholastic forms — *bhāṣya*, *prakaraṇa*, catechetical verse with auto-commentary — by which one is recognised as a legitimate *sampradāya* in late-medieval inter-Vedāntic discourse. The *Govinda-Bhāṣya*'s successful presentation at the eighteenth-century Galta debate and the consequent admission of the Gauḍīyas to the recognised *catur-sampradāya* register the achievement institutionally. The transition completed under Baladeva is from a tradition organised around Vraja-experience and *bhakti-rasa* into one that also meets the standard expectations of pan-Indian Vedāntic legitimacy on those expectations' own terms.

engaged_works.title: Govinda-Bhashya
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored formal Gauḍīya Brahma-Sūtra commentary.
engaged_works.summary: The *Govinda-Bhāṣya* is the Gauḍīya school's formal Brahma-Sūtra commentary. Baladeva composed it in eighteenth-century Jaipur in direct response to a challenge from the Rāmānanda-Sampradāya at the Galta court: the Caitanya-Vaiṣṇavas could not be admitted to the company of legitimate *sampradāyas*, the challenge ran, because they lacked a *bhāṣya* on the *Brahma-Sūtras*. The work is therefore both a doctrinal text and a sectarian credential, and the institutional stakes inflect the bhāṣya's organisation. Sectarian tradition reports that Baladeva produced the work rapidly under inspiration from Govindajī himself; the story is part of the school's self-understanding and explains the title. Doctrinally each *adhikaraṇa* is read through *acintya-bhedābheda*: the threefold *śakti*-doctrine, Kṛṣṇa as *svayaṃ-bhagavān*, the *jīva* as *taṭastha-aṃśa*, and *bhakti–prema* as the operative path. The Madhva-inflected emphasis on the irreducibility of *bheda* gives the work its characteristic flavour and distinguishes it from the Gosvāmī systematic theology that more often emphasised the *acintya* qualifier as such. The successful presentation at Galta secured Gauḍīya admission to the recognised *catur-sampradāya* lineage and is therefore an institutional milestone in the school's history as much as a doctrinal text.

engaged_works.title: Siddhanta-Ratnam
engaged_works.summary: The *Siddhānta-Ratnam* (sometimes circulated as the *Vaiṣṇava-Nandinī* tradition's doctrinal counterpart) is Baladeva's compendium of settled Gauḍīya philosophical positions, presented in the manner of a *prameya-saṅgraha*: the topics that any classical Vedānta school must take a stance on — *pramāṇa*-theory, the nature of *Brahman*, the *jīva*, *jagat*, the *Brahman*–*jīva*–*jagat* relation, *bandha* and *mokṣa*, the *upāya* and the *upeya* — are stated, defended, and demarcated against rival schools (Advaita, Viśiṣṭādvaita, Madhva-Dvaita, Nimbārka, and the *bhedābheda* lines). Where the *Govinda-Bhāṣya* is constrained by *sūtra*-form, the *Siddhānta-Ratnam* allows Baladeva to set out the Gauḍīya *acintya-bhedābheda* synoptically and polemically. It functions as the school's portable doctrinal reference and as the systematic supplement to the bhāṣya.

engaged_works.title: Prameya-Ratnavali
engaged_works.ascription_notes: Securely authored summary of Gauḍīya prameyas.
engaged_works.summary: The *Prameya-Ratnāvalī* ("jewelled necklace of established truths") is Baladeva's tightly compressed catechetical summary of the nine Gauḍīya *prameyas*: the supremacy of Kṛṣṇa as *svayaṃ-bhagavān*; his being known by all the *Vedas*; the *Bhāgavata-Purāṇa* as the supreme *pramāṇa*; *bhakti* alone as *abhidheya*; *prema* as *prayojana*; the *jīva*'s natural condition as eternal servant of Kṛṣṇa; the simultaneous *bheda* and *abheda* of *jīva*–*Brahman* by *acintya-śakti*; the world as Bhagavān's real *pariṇāma* through *māyā-śakti*; and the *guru-paramparā*. The text is structured in clear verses with auto-commentary suitable for memorisation and instruction, and explicitly identifies its lineage with Madhva, embedding the Gauḍīya school within the *brahma-mādhva-gauḍīya* paramparā as Baladeva understands it. Together with the *Govinda-Bhāṣya*, it establishes the standard Gauḍīya doctrinal profile presented in inter-sectarian Vedāntic discourse.
